title: JIT capability composition + sandbox for subagent dispatch — operation-granular tool+perm grants from an empty-state registry
description: The safety/capability layer that sits atop FEAT-004 (agents-as-tools dispatch). FEAT-004 makes a subagent invocable as a typed tool call by the main context agent; THIS feature governs WHAT that subagent may do. Model (user-articulated 2026-05-24): a subagent is a programmatic action — a tool call carrying atomic context chunks composed into prompt shape (already supported: pi-jit-agents compile boundary + contextBlocks + introspect agentContract tool-shape projection). The subagent receives its OWN tools, JIT-composed per invocation, scoped to EXACTLY the operations the task needs and no others — e.g. the right to run a specific command (`npm test`) rather than the `bash` tool wholesale; a specific file path to read/write rather than read/write generally. Default is EMPTY: no perms, no tools; configs + schemas + macros materialize the exact capability grant from empty state (generalizing DEC-0015/0011/0025 empty-state-config-driven philosophy from the substrate dir to the agent/tool/perm surface). Capabilities drawn from a tool+permission REGISTRY, atomically composable into a per-dispatch bundle, and ideally sandboxed (fs/network jail around the subprocess). Pairs with DEC-0047 (constitutional capability model) which makes the registry + its composition human-ratified config (legislative), the main agent's dispatch executive, and validators/monitors judicial.
motivation: FEAT-004 bare dispatch is necessary but not sufficient for a tools-constrained autonomous orchestrator: letting the main agent self-orchestrate (dispatch coding/explore/adversarial subagents that edit source, run shell, etc.) is only SAFE if each subagent's authority is exactly scoped, minimal, and human-sanctioned. Operation-granular JIT capability composition + sandboxing is the property that converts 'self-orchestration is possible' into 'self-orchestration is trustworthy'. Today tool restriction is category-granular only (`--tools read`), there is no permission/capability registry, and no per-invocation composition or sandbox.
acceptance_criteria: A capability/permission registry exists (config-declared, empty by default) whose entries are tool grants AND operation-level permissions (e.g. an allowlisted command, a path-scoped read/write), not just tool categories
Per subagent-dispatch, a capability bundle is composed JUST-IN-TIME from the registry, granting exactly the declared operations and no others; the subagent cannot exceed its bundle
Empty-state default: a subagent with no declared capabilities can do nothing; all capability arrives via config/schema/macro composition (no ambient tools/perms)
Dispatch path enforces the bundle (subprocess --tools/perm scoping and/or in-process tool-subset gating via the FEAT-004 execute boundary); enforcement is verifiable
Sandboxing option: the dispatched subprocess can be confined (fs/network) to its declared scope
Dual-surface per DEC-0019/0020 (library + Pi tool + CLI) so both the in-pi main agent and the orchestrator-side compose capabilities the same way
The composition + the registry are config the human ratifies (DEC-0047 constitutional gate); the main agent proposes capability mutations, never self-grants unratified
